ddls:
CREATE TABLE "threads" (
  "thread_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each thread */,
  "thread_name" TEXT /* Name of the thread */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the device the thread is running on */,
  "stack_usage" INTEGER /* Current stack usage in bytes */,
  "stack_allocated" INTEGER /* Total stack allocated in bytes */,
  "unused_stack" REAL /* Percentage of unused stack */,
  "cpu_usage" REAL /* CPU usage as a percentage */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date and time when the thread data was last updated */,
  "thread_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the thread's purpose or functionality */,
  "thread_priority" TEXT /* Priority level of the thread (e.g., high, medium, low) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("thread_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_threads_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "devices" (
  "device_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each device */,
  "device_name" TEXT /* Name of the device */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of the device (e.g., industrial control, automotive) */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the device */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date the device was installed */,
  "last_maintenance" TEXT /* Date of the last maintenance */,
  "device_serial_number" TEXT /* Unique serial number of the device */,
  "device_model" TEXT /* Model number or type of the device */,
  "device_manufacturer" TEXT /* Name of the manufacturer for the device */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "thread_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "thread_id" INTEGER /* ID of the thread the log entry belongs to */,
  "stack_usage" INTEGER /* Stack usage in bytes at the time of logging */,
  "stack_allocated" INTEGER /* Stack allocated in bytes at the time of logging */,
  "unused_stack" REAL /* Percentage of unused stack at the time of logging */,
  "cpu_usage" REAL /* CPU usage as a percentage at the time of logging */,
  "log_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the log entry was created */,
  "log_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the log entry */,
  "log_tags" TEXT /* Tags or keywords associated with the log entry */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_thread_logs_thread_id FOREIGN KEY ("thread_id") REFERENCES threads ("thread_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "thread_id" INTEGER /* ID of the thread the alert is related to */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of the alert (e.g., high CPU usage, low stack) */,
  "alert_message" TEXT /* Message describing the alert */,
  "alert_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the alert was generated */,
  "resolved" INTEGER /* Flag indicating whether the alert has been resolved */,
  "alert_severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the alert (e.g., critical, warning, info) */,
  "alert_acknowledged" INTEGER /* Flag indicating whether the alert has been acknowledged */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_thread_id FOREIGN KEY ("thread_id") REFERENCES threads ("thread_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, engineer, support) */,
  "user_phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "user_department" TEXT /* Department or team the user belongs to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the system */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the system was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., login, logout, view, edit) */,
  "access_ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access event originated */,
  "access_user_agent" TEXT /* Information about the user agent used for the access event */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "maintenance_records" (
  "record_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each maintenance record */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device the maintenance was performed on */,
  "maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date when the maintenance was performed */,
  "maintenance_type" TEXT /* Type of maintenance (e.g., routine, emergency) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the maintenance performed */,
  "performed_by" TEXT /* Name of the person who performed the maintenance */,
  "maintenance_cost" REAL /* Cost associated with the maintenance */,
  "maintenance_duration" REAL /* Duration of the maintenance in hours */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("record_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_maintenance_records_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "device_configurations" (
  "config_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each configuration setting */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device the configuration setting belongs to */,
  "config_key" TEXT /* Key of the configuration setting */,
  "config_value" TEXT /* Value of the configuration setting */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date and time when the configuration was last updated */,
  "config_category" TEXT /* Category or group of the configuration setting */,
  "config_notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the configuration setting */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("config_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_device_configurations_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id")
)
